response.justification: The candidate is an experienced senior full-stack web developer with a strong software craftsmanship background, but their experience is almost entirely in web, mobile, and product engineering (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang web services). The role requires SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture knowledge, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appear in the resume. Beyond brief low-latency systems work and some C/C++ tooling experience, there is no evidence of hardware architecture, deep-learning workload optimization, or performance modeling at the silicon level expected of a Principal Performance Architect.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience, exceeding the BS + 8 years requirement
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec in DLang with async I/O, fibers, caching)
Some C/C++ and embedded/hardware-adjacent exposure (National Instruments, robotic arms, audio wave field synthesis thesis)
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, or AI accelerator experience whatsoever
No deep neural network training/inference optimization or SIMD/MIMD architecture background
Career trajectory is web/product engineering, not hardware performance architecture — a fundamental domain mismatch for a Principal-level IC role

key_factors.factor: Strong general engineering tenure and low-latency systems work
